title: alchemilla pubescens subsp. splendens
intro: Alchemilla pubescens subsp. splendens, also known as the Alpine Lady's Mantle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its rosettes of leaves and yellow-green flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and polyphenolic compounds. The health benefits of Alchemilla pubescens subsp. splendens include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant in tinctures and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
